item.title: Content Manager - Local Government
item.description: https://ckrecruit.co.uk/job/content-manager-local-government/ Make an impact through communications that shape the future of public services. This is an exciting opportunity for a strategic yet hands-on communications professional who thrives on delivering engaging campaigns, managing high-profile events, and building meaningful stakeholder engagement. Working closely with the Communications Director, you'll play a pivotal role in strengthening the organisations brand, leading integrated communications activity, and delivering a varied programme of in-person and digital events. This is a varied role that combines strategic planning with practical delivery, including supporting events on-site and producing high-quality communications that enhance our profile and impact. What you'll be doing Lead integrated communications and marketing campaigns. Manage the organisation's programme of conferences, stakeholder events, webinars and launches. Create engaging content for websites, newsletters, social media, articles and press releases. Build strong relationships with senior stakeholders, partners and government organisations. Coordinate suppliers, venues, logistics and digital event platforms. Identify opportunities to improve communications, engagement and organisational impact. About you You'll be an experienced communications, marketing or events professional who enjoys balancing strategic thinking with practical delivery. You'll have: Significant experience delivering integrated communications and marketing campaigns. Outstanding writing and editing skills. Experience managing websites, social media, CMS and CRM platforms. Excellent organisational skills with the ability to manage multiple priorities. Confidence working with senior stakeholders and external partners. A proactive, collaborative and solutions-focused approach. If you're an organised, creative communications professional who enjoys delivering high-quality campaigns and events get in touch! Carolyn@ckrecruit.co.uk Closing Date : 18 August 2026 Interview/Start Dates N/A Application Details https://ckrecruit.co.uk/job/content-manager-local-government/ Website : http://ckrecruit.co.uk

item.title: Campaigns Officer (Tiverton)
item.description: We’re looking for an enthusiastic, driven, and organised individual to support our campaigning, recruitment, and capacity-building in the run-up to Devon’s local elections in 2027. Alongside our Campaign Team and Regional Campaign Manager, this role will see you working right at the heart of local politics. You’ll be part of an ambitious team determined to ‘put more gold on the map’, helping to advance council campaigns by working closely with councillors, candidates, and volunteers to deliver campaigning on the ground and to develop and grow our team. If you’re a proactive and people-oriented person, excited by the opportunity to help grow, build, and lead our team, then we’d love to hear from you. Full details are available on our website at https://www.libdems.org.uk/work-for-us/vacancy/campaigns-officer-tiverton Closing Date : 29 July 2026 Interview/Start Dates The closing time is 5pm, with interviews to be held as soon as possible following closing - though please note that we will be reviewing applications as they come in and reserve the right to interview and appoint prior to the closing date. Application Details Please apply by email to Todd Olive, Campaigns Manager (Devon & Cornwall), at todd.olive@libdems.org.uk with the subject line “Application – Tiverton Campaigns Officer”, attaching: Your CV of no more than two sides of A4 , including contact details for two recent referees A cover letter of no more than one side of A4 setting out why you are applying and any relevant organising experience. Should you wish to arrange an informal discussion about the role, please get in touch with Todd Olive, Campaigns Manager (Devon & Cornwall), at todd.olive@libdems.org.uk Website : https://www.rachelgilmour.org.uk/
item.dc:creator: Honiton, Sidmouth & Tiverton Liberal Democrats

item.title: Parliamentary Assistant
item.description: Satvir Kaur MP is seeking to recruit a Parliamentary Assistant for her Westminster office to cover Maternity Leave. Key Responsibilities Research local, regional, or national issues to support the MP’s work. To produce and present high-quality written reports, speeches, briefing notes, and professional advice Deal with complex and sensitive queries and correspondence on MP’s behalf, including drafting and signing letters. Respond to routine correspondence and enquiries from constituents, media, lobbyists, and pressure groups. Horizon-scanning, anticipate what advice or action may be required; proactively identify problems and resolve them Attend, participate in and take action notes at meetings as appropriate Diary Management in support of the Office Manager Engage confidentially, professionally, with care and integrity with members of the public, through telephone enquiries, social media, email and face to face Monitor local, regional and national media coverage and brief the MP on relevant issues Progress casework as required Project Work Provide generalist admin support including acting as first point of contact for visitors and constituency groups visiting Parliament There may be a requirement for the role holder to work outside of normal working hours and travel for work occasionally, including regular visits to the constituency office Skills & Experience: Willingness to learn and undertake training A high standard of written work An interest in current affairs and policy issues A can-do and proactive attitude Personable and able to communicate with people from all backgrounds Excellent attention to detail Experienced working as part of a team. This is a London based role, applicants must be able to work from Parliament Upon appointment you will be required to comply with the Baseline Personnel Security Standard, undertaken by the Members’ Staff Verification Office (MSVO). See Members’ Staff Verification Office (MSVO) page for further info. MPs generally pay staff in accordance with IPSA guidelines . Closing Date : 27 July 2026 Interview/Start Dates Closing time for applications 4pm. Interviews: Provisional date 4th August Start Date: mid-September subject to vetting Application Details To apply, please send your CV and covering letter to Karen Baker, Office Manager for Satvir Kaur MP, karen.baker@parliament.uk Website : http://satvirkaur.info
item.dc:creator: Satvir Kaur MP (Southampton Test)

item.title: Director, Global Governance and Strategic Engagement
item.description: The Security Futures Lab, hosted by the Oxford Martin School, University of Oxford, was established to do one thing: rethink how security is understood and governed in an age of cascading, systemic risk. We are looking for a Director, Glob...

item.title: Communications Assistant
item.description: Lee Pitcher MP is seeking a highly motivated individual with excellent organisation and communication skills, including skills in digital and video production and photography to support his work in Doncaster East and the Isle of Axholme. This post is available for a six-month fixed term, 22 hours per week. Duties include, but are not limited to: Producing clear, engaging and creative communications content across multiple platforms - including photos and videos for use in effectively communicating Lee's work with constituents Supporting the wider team to develop an engaging communications plan and deliver assigned actions Accompaying Lee to constituency events and ensuring the best possible photos and videos are captured Regularly respond to incoming constituent correspondence - specifically policy campaign responses Other duties as may be reasonably directed by the Office Manager Person Specification Essential: An 'eye' for good visual content and the confidence to get it Excellent interpersonal skills and social confidence in order to engage with multiple people and get the content needed Creativity, self motivation and enthusiasm Excellent written and verbal communication skills, and an ability to tailor content for the correct audience A strong awareness of current affairs and the political landscape - and a strong understanding of the character and priorities of Doncaster East and the Isle of Axholme Desirable: Experience in previous digital communications roles or similar Experience using Canva, Adobe Suite or similar Sympathy with the aims and values of the Labour Party Upon appointment you will be required to comply with the Baseline Personnel Security Standard, undertaken by the Members’ Staff Verification Office (MSVO). See Members’ Staff Verification Office (MSVO) page for further info. MPs generally pay staff in accordance with IPSA guidelines . Closing Date : 26 July 2026 Interview/Start Dates Interviews in week commencing 27th July. Start date will be subject to completion of a successful security vetting process, and the start date cannot be guaranteed before this is complete. Application Details To apply please email a covering letter and CV to Lee's Office Manager at gary.barratt@parliament.uk with 'Comms Assistant Application' in the subject line. Website : http://www.leepitcher.org.uk
item.dc:creator: Lee Pitcher MP (Doncaster East and the Isle of Axholme)

item.title: Head of the Mayor's Office
item.description: As the Mayor's most senior advisor, the Head of the Mayor’s plays a pivotal role in translating political ambition into actionable policies, ensuring seamless alignment between policy execution and operational oversight. This position guarantees that the Mayor's priorities are effectively implemented by the Mayor’s Office, the Combined Authority, and external partners. As the primary point of contact for stakeholders engaging with the Mayor, the Head of the Mayor’s Office ensures the Mayor's agenda is executed with precision, pace, and impact. This role demands a profound understanding of public affairs, operations, and policy delivery, providing strategic counsel to the Mayor and ensuring the smooth functioning of the office. Moreover, the Head of the Mayor’s Office is tasked with articulating the Mayor's vision to partners and stakeholders, ensuring it resonates across the region. This involves collaborating with the Combined Authority and external organisations to maximise alignment with the Mayor's priorities and strategically utilising the Mayor's time to drive impactful results. What you will bring. Leadership & Oversight: direct and manage the Mayor’s agenda, ensuring effective implementation of the Mayor’s vision through policy development, operational delivery, and public affairs. Strategic Coordination: ensure the Combined Authority, local authorities, and regional partners align with the Mayor’s priorities. Stakeholder Management: Leading, negotiating and influencing a wide range of stakeholders including those at a senior level, including building and growing partnerships with central Government Departments. Operational Delivery: ensure effective execution of Mayoral commitments, tracking progress, identifying risks, and implementing mitigation strategies. Public Affairs & Policy Execution: engage with government officials, Ministers, regional politicians and public sector partners to advance policy ambitions, lobbying for regional interests and securing investment. Communication: work with internal and external stakeholders to ensure the Mayor’s vision is well-articulated and that engagements, meetings, media appearances and events contribute meaningfully to the delivery of his agenda. Politically Restricted Post Closing Date : 26 July 2026 Interview/Start Dates Interview date: 5, 6 or 7 August 2026 Application Details https://nycjobs.engageats.co.uk/Vacancies/I/3501/0/471514/23664/head-of-the-mayor-s-office Website : https://yorknorthyorks-ca.gov.uk/
item.dc:creator: York and North Yorkshire Combine Authorty

item.title: Conference Assistants (x4)
item.description: Location: Head Office - Newcastle The Labour Party is looking to recruit 4x Conference Assistants. The post-holders will be responsible for providing full administrative and events support to the Conference Team. Customer services to all applicants to National Events and Conferences. The successful candidate will have: • Knowledge of the aims and structure of the Labour Party • Administration experience in a busy and fast paced environment • Ability to provide excellent customer service to all conference visitors and delegates • Ability to work in an environment which involves the use and/or dissemination of confidential information Applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis and we may choose to close the vacancy before this date if suitable candidates are identified. The Labour Party values equality and diversity, and welcomes applications from candidates regardless of sex, race, disability, age, sexual orientation, gender reassignment, religion or belief, marriage and civil partnership, or pregnancy and maternity. Closing Date : 26 July 2026 Interview/Start Dates Interview dates to be confirmed. Application Details To find out more and apply to this vacancy, please see the original listing on our careers site: https://api.occupop.com/shared/job/conference-assistants-x4-baa18/W4MP Website : http://www.labour.org.uk
item.dc:creator: The Labour Party

item.title: Account Executive
item.description: What we’re looking for • Excellent writing skills, with the ability to turn complex information into clear, accessible and engaging copy for different audiences. • A genuine interest in current affairs, politics, stakeholder engagement, environmental issues and sustainability. • A confident organiser who can help plan and deliver consultation events and engagement activity, often working to tight deadlines, coordinating logistics and equipment, and keeping things moving calmly when plans change. • A positive can-do attitude, good initiative and the ability to roll your sleeves up and manage a varied workload that can change at short notice. • Experience of, or a strong interest in, research, analysing feedback and data, and supporting the preparation of reports. • Confidence in building strong working relationships with clients, community representatives, elected members, local organisations and stakeholders. • Good judgement, attention to detail and an understanding of risk, with the confidence to think through challenges, suggest solutions and challenge how things are done when there is a better way. • Creative flair, curiosity and an appetite to continually improve how we work, communicate and engage with communities. • Digital confidence, including an understanding of how social media and digital channels can support engagement. • As many of our campaigns and projects are bilingual, Welsh language skills — speaking, reading and writing — are desirable. Rewards and benefits package • 24 days leave rising to 29 days with length of service (plus bank holidays) • Hybrid working 2 to 3 days a week from our Cardiff office. • Flexible work approach with core hours. • Two paid volunteering days a year to help your local community. • Two weeks paid sabbatical every three years. • Workplace pension scheme. • Health Cash Plan • Training and CPD. Closing Date : 26 July 2026 Interview/Start Dates The interview date is Thursday 30 July 2026. Application Details To apply, email your CV with a short covering letter explaining your interest in the role to recruitment@grasshopper-comms.co.uk If you would like an informal chat about the role, please contact: recruitment@grasshopper-comms.co.uk Website : http://grasshopper-comms.co.uk Additional Notes 16 July: salary was in error. Updated from £24,242 to £25,878
item.dc:creator: Grasshopper Communications

item.title: Communications Officer — Maternity Cover
item.description: Luke Myer MP is looking for a creative and reliable Communications Officer to support his constituency and parliamentary communications during a period of maternity cover. This is a part-time role, two days a week, with a strong focus on video editing, short-form digital content and social media . The role can be carried out remotely or from the constituency office in Guisborough. Duties will include, but are not limited to: Editing short-form video content for social media, including captions, subtitles and basic formatting for different platforms. Producing graphics, photographs and digital assets using tools such as Canva, Adobe Premiere Pro, CapCut, Photoshop or similar. Drafting and scheduling social media posts across platforms. Supporting the office’s wider online presence, including website updates and email newsletters where required. Turning casework themes, constituency visits, parliamentary activity and local campaigns into clear, resident-facing content. Ensuring content is accurate, accessible, professional and appropriate for a parliamentary office. Working with colleagues to gather footage, quotes, local information and updates. Supporting occasional press, media or stakeholder communications. Ensuring records, content and information are handled confidentially and in line with data protection requirements. Other reasonable duties as directed by the Office Manager or MP. Person Specification Essential Good video editing skills, especially for short-form social media content. Experience creating digital content for Instagram, Facebook, TikTok, X/Twitter or similar platforms. Good political judgement and an understanding of how to communicate sensitively on local and national issues. Ability to work independently, meet deadlines and manage a small but varied workload. Confidence using digital tools such as Canva, CapCut, Adobe Premiere Pro, Photoshop or similar. Understanding of confidentiality, data protection and the need for professionalism in an MP’s office. Desirable Experience working in politics, Parliament, local government, trade unions, campaigns, charities or community organisations. Understanding of Middlesbrough and East Cleveland, or the wider Tees Valley. Experience preparing content from raw footage. Sympathy with the aims and values of the Labour Party. Upon appointment you will be required to comply with the Baseline Personnel Security Standard, undertaken by the Members’ Staff Verification Office (MSVO). See Members’ Staff Verification Office (MSVO) page for further info. MPs generally pay staff in accordance with IPSA guidelines . Closing Date : 27 July 2026 Interview/Start Dates Please apply early as we may appoint before the closing date. Interview dates: Week starting 27th July Starting date: August Application Details Please email: CV (max 2 pages) Cover letter (max 1 page) explaining your interest and what you can bring to the role Email to: Luke.Myer.MP@Parliament.uk Subject: “Communications Officer – [Your Name]” Website : http://LukeMyer.uk
item.dc:creator: Luke Myer MP (Middlesbrough South and East Cleveland)

item.title: Admin Officer — Maternity Cover
item.description: Luke Myer MP is looking for an organised and reliable Admin Officer to support the smooth running of his constituency office during a period of maternity cover. This is a part-time role, based in the constituency office for two days per week . The successful candidate will provide general administrative support to the team, helping ensure that office systems, records and day-to-day processes are well managed. Key Responsibilities Duties will include, but are not limited to: Providing general administrative support to the constituency office. Answering telephone calls, taking messages and helping direct enquiries appropriately. Supporting the team with diary management, meeting arrangements and appointments. Maintaining accurate office records, filing systems and databases. Assisting with the organisation of advice surgeries, meetings and constituency events. Supporting the Office Manager in the day-to-day running of the office. Helping ensure casework and correspondence are logged and processed appropriately. Handling confidential information carefully and in line with data protection requirements. Supporting colleagues with other reasonable administrative duties as required. Person Specification Essential Strong organisational and administrative skills. Good written and verbal communication skills. Good attention to detail. Ability to manage competing tasks and work to deadlines. Confidence using Microsoft Office, email systems and basic office software. A professional, discreet and helpful manner. Ability to deal sensitively with members of the public. Understanding of confidentiality and data protection. Ability to work as part of a small team. Desirable Experience working in an office, administrative or customer-facing role. Experience using a casework system, CRM or database. Experience in a volunteer led organisation Understanding of the role of an MP’s constituency office. Knowledge of Middlesbrough and East Cleveland, or the wider Tees Valley. Sympathy with the aims and values of the Labour Party. Upon appointment you will be required to comply with the Baseline Personnel Security Standard, undertaken by the Members’ Staff Verification Office (MSVO). See Members’ Staff Verification Office (MSVO) page for further info. MPs generally pay staff in accordance with IPSA guidelines . Closing Date : 27 July 2026 Interview/Start Dates Interviews: week starting 27th July Start date: August Application Details Please email: CV (max 2 pages) Cover letter (max 1 page) explaining your interest and what you can bring to the role Email to: Luke.Myer.MP@Parliament.uk Subject: “Communications Intern Application – [Your Name]” Website : http://LukeMyer.uk
item.dc:creator: Luke Myer MP (Middlesbrough South and East Cleveland)

item.title: Constituency Assistant and Communications Officer
item.description: Job Details • Deputise for the Parliamentary Assistant during periods of absence • Support the work of the Parliamentary Assistant and work closely with them. • Plan and manage the MP's constituency visit programme, including identifying potential visits, responding to visit requests and agreeing with the MP the prioritisation of visits. • Maintain positive on-going relationships with individuals and organisations requesting or involved with arranging a visit from the MP. • Maintain the MP's constituency calendar. • Assist the constituency casework team by arranging time for the MP to meet with constituents where necessary to progress casework • Make arrangements for visits to have appropriate briefing material, staffing and security • Engage confidentially, professionally, with care and integrity with members of the public, through telephone enquiries, social media, email and face to face. • Provide generalist admin support, including checking parliamentary inbox Key responsibilities — Communications Officer • Plan the MP's communications, preparing press releases and online content as required to meet publication deadlines (on constituency, non-party political matters). Often working closely with the Parliamentary Assistant. • Monitor media coverage and liaise with media agencies. • Establish, monitor and update a social media and online presence in the constituency. Produce online graphics and video content, as well as publicising surgeries and news alerts etc. • Manage and monitor social media enquiries, dealing with cases that do not need to be transferred to a higher level. Ensure all enquiries are dealt with in a timely manner Key responsibilities • Support the work of the MP. • Attend constituency meetings as appropriate • There may be a requirement for the role holder to work outside of the normal working hours and travel for work occasionally. Whilst based in the constituency, the role will require occasional work in Westminster. Upon appointment you will be required to comply with the Baseline Personnel Security Standard, undertaken by the Members’ Staff Verification Office (MSVO). See Members’ Staff Verification Office (MSVO) page for further info. MPs generally pay staff in accordance with IPSA guidelines . Closing Date : 5 August 2026 Interview/Start Dates We reserve the right to close applications early Interview/Start Dates: 10th August Application Details Application Detail: Email Chris on Chris.ward@parliament.uk Please send a CV and a one page A4 covering letter. Please title email *name of candidate* Comms role Website : http://Wokinghamlibdems.org.uk
item.dc:creator: Clive Jones MP (Wokingham)

item.title: Events Officer
item.description: We are seeking a bright and hardworking person to help our events team organise interesting, informative and impactful discussions between leading policy experts and high-profile politicians. Reporting to the Acting Head of Events and Operations, you will help us arrange panel discussions, speeches and policy roundtables, which take place online, in-person and hybrid. You will also contribute to our busy programme across the Liberal Democrat, Labour and Conservative Party Conferences in the autumn. Speakers at SMF events in 2025 have included a number of high-profile politicians including the Chancellor of the Exchequer, the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, the pensions minister and a shadow health minister. Topics covered have included pensions, social mobility, gambling, misinformation, health inequalities and AI. Apply here: https://www.smf.co.uk/new-opportunity-events-officer-2/?_gl=1*kjnasn*_ga*NTIzNDA4MjgzLjE3ODQwMjI1MDg.*_up*MQ..*_ga_H0NEYP8C68*czE3ODQwMjI1MTAkbzEkZzAkdDE3ODQwMjI1MTAkajYwJGwwJGgw Closing Date : 31 July 2026 Interview/Start Dates Applications close at 9am. Shortlisted candidates will be invited to an initial interview in person at the SMF’s offices in Westminster and may be asked to complete a short written task in advance. Application Details Application details here: https://www.smf.co.uk/new-opportunity-events-officer-2/?_gl=1*kjnasn*_ga*NTIzNDA4MjgzLjE3ODQwMjI1MDg.*_up*MQ..*_ga_H0NEYP8C68*czE3ODQwMjI1MTAkbzEkZzAkdDE3ODQwMjI1MTAkajYwJGwwJGgw Website : http://www.smf.co.uk
item.dc:creator: Social Market Foundation

item.title: Communications Officer
item.description: Andrew Lewin MP is looking for a Communications Officer who understands politics, writes persuasively and excels in the creation of social media content. Joining his team in Westminster, the focus of the role will be to lead on all elements of communications, ensuring that Andrew’s campaigns, work in Welwyn Hatfield and Westminster reach a wide audience in his constituency. The successful candidate will also be expected to support existing members of the team in responding to policy casework on national issues. Communications Experience of creating and editing short films (30 seconds to 2 minutes) is essential. So too will be graphic design skills, ideally with experience of using tools including: Photoshop or Canva Editing software such as Da Vinci Resolve or Capcut Subtitling software such as VEED The successful candidate will need to create and execute complementary plans for multiple social media platforms, informed by evidence and to meet specific local objectives. Content will include: Promoting Andrew’s work in the constituency and amplifying campaigns in Welwyn Hatfield Video content summarising key debates and speeches in parliament and how it impacts local people Promoting Andrew’s local engagements and driving attendance at events like coffee mornings Managing the website, which is to be frequently updated with a broad range of content They will also support the MP with his monthly e-newsletter, fortnightly column in the local paper and as appropriate, liaison with journalists. The successful candidate will also work with the team to organise and coordinate Andrew’s visits in Welwyn Hatfield. Policy Alongside this role the successful candidate will also be expected to support the wider team on policy casework and drafting responses to constituents. They will analyse incoming correspondence and articulate findings in communication with the MP and other staff members. Details The role will be based in Westminster with an average of 3 days a month in Welwyn Hatfield. There will also be an occasional requirement for the role holder to support events and visits on Saturday mornings or weekday evenings in Welwyn Hatfield. Upon appointment you will be required to comply with the Baseline Personnel Security Standard, undertaken by the Members’ Staff Verification Office (MSVO). See Members’ Staff Verification Office (MSVO) page for further info. MPs generally pay staff in accordance with IPSA guidelines . Closing Date : 24 July 2026 Interview/Start Dates Interviews will be held on the 4th of August. Candidates who are unable to make that date should note this in their application so alternative arrangements can be made. The successful candidate will start in September, subject to satisfactory references and parliamentary security clearance. Application Details Send your CV and a covering letter of no more than 500 words, explaining how you meet the requirements of the role, to josh.lee@parliament.uk Candidates are also welcome to submit a portfolio of work including but not limited to a graphic and short form video. Website : http://www.andrewlewin.co.uk
item.dc:creator: Andrew Lewin MP (Welwyn Hatfield)

item.title: Public Affairs Officer (16-month FTC)
item.description: Full time (35 hours per week), 16-month contract with possible extension Hybrid working, minimum 2 days per week in the London office Parkinson’s UK Our client, Parkinson’s UK, is looking for an enthusiastic and well organised public affairs professional, with a good knowledge of how policy change works in Westminster and Whitehall. You’ll support their work to raise the profile of Parkinson’s UK, increase awareness of Parkinson’s and build strong relationships with Parliamentarians and civil servants. Supporting the policy advisers, you’ll keep a close eye on developments in Parliament and support colleagues in the devolved nations in their Parliamentary work. What you'll do: Monitor activity in Parliament that relates to Parkinson’s and share information with colleagues Draft briefings and materials to support their work to build effective relationships with key parliamentarians, influencers and civil servants Provide support to the All-Party Parliamentary Group on Parkinson’s Support colleagues in devolved nations to ensure their public affairs work is aligned and supportive across the UK Support work to involve people with lived experience in the charity’s public affairs activities Produce inspiring communications about the organisation’s parliamentary activities that encourage their audiences to continue supporting their work Build a strong network with allies and partners in other charities and external groups What you'll bring: Experience of working in Westminster or Whitehall or in the devolved nations’ assemblies Good interpersonal skills and the ability to provide administrative support Experience in political analysis and of working in a political environment and a good understanding of policy issues in health and care, welfare, work, transport or the disability arena Ability to network and build supportive relationships at all levels, including with volunteers Experience of using creative ways of communicating messages to Parliamentarians and civil servants Research, analytical and information gathering skills Ability to maintain political impartiality Ability to prioritise own workload effectively and support others in meeting challenging deadlines. Closing Date : 28 July 2026 Interview/Start Dates Closing time: 12pm Interviews : Wednesday 5 August and Friday 7 August (online) Start date : As soon as possible. Due to timing, we are ideally looking for candidates with no longer than one month's notice Application Details To be considered, please send a copy of your CV, together with a supporting statement (max 2 sides of A4) fully demonstrating how you meet all the criteria of the role, as stated in the "What you'll bring" section of the job description via https://www.whitonmaynard.com/public-affairs-officer.html Please note that this is not an entry-level role. We can only consider candidates who can clearly demonstrate experience working within a public affairs/political environment. For example, you might be working for an MP, or within a charity/NGO influencing team or a public affairs agency. Website : https://www.parkinsons.org.uk/
item.dc:creator: Parkinson's UK

item.title: Parliamentary Assistant
item.description: The successful candidate will support Sir Jeremy’s work in the Westminster office. An ideal candidate will have previous Parliamentary experience and have a good knowledge of local issues and campaigns which Sir Jeremy is involved with. The candidate will be willing to work hard, learn from others, and be a good team player. Responsibilities include, but are not limited to: • Drafting articles • Lead on social media • Completing research projects on a wide variety of topics • Deal with a large number of queries and campaigns, including policy and casework matters • Organising Parliamentary tours and PMQ tickets for constituents • Monitoring media, press and the progress of legislation • Conducting and maintaining general correspondence and general office administration • Attend surgeries and meetings in Westminster and the constituency (as agreed in advance) Experience required: • Good political awareness and judgement • Effective policy correspondence management • Excellent oral and written communication skills • Analytical skills and attention to detail • Strong organisational skills • Experience in managing and developing social media accounts • Researching, using Parliamentary resources • Ability to work independently and as part of a team • Previous Parliamentary experience is desirable • Sympathetic to the aims and values of the Conservative Party Upon appointment you will be required to comply with the Baseline Personnel Security Standard, undertaken by the Members’ Staff Verification Office (MSVO). See Members’ Staff Verification Office (MSVO) page for further info. MPs generally pay staff in accordance with IPSA guidelines . Closing Date : 31 July 2026 Interview/Start Dates Interviews will take place as soon as possible with a start date commencing at the beginning of September. Application Details Please email CV and covering letter to jessica.eglin@parliament.uk Website : http://www.jeremywright.org.uk
item.dc:creator: Sir Jeremy Wright KC MP (Kenilworth & Southam)

item.title: Policy and Public Affairs Officer
item.description: Our BHF Policy and Public Affairs team exists to build support amongst key decision makers so that we can beat heart disease for good. We develop BHF’s policy position and engage and influence stakeholders to help stop heart disease before it starts, save lives and support those living with heart disease to live longer, healthier lives. In this role, you’ll promote BHF to politicians, civil servants, researchers and the clinical community in Wales, helping to ensure that key decision-makers consider BHF the expert on heart health and research. You’ll influence the Welsh Government and other key stakeholders on our influencing priorities, making cardiovascular disease a priority in Wales as well as ensure Wales has an environment which supports excellence in heart research. Working arrangements This is a hybrid role, where your work will be split between your home and at least one day per week, on average, in our Cardiff Office. This may vary from time to time, so you will need to work in a flexible way to unlock your best work for our cause. About you With knowledge and understanding of the political environment in Wales, you’ll also have knowledge of the workings and processes of devolved government in Wales. You’ll join BHF Cymru at an exciting time for both the charity and heart health in Wales and will play a key role in the work BHF does and the impact it is having on the lives of everyone in Wales and the UK. To be successful in this role you’ll have the following skills and experience: • An interest in working in policy and/or public affairs in Wales • A willingness to learn and implement effective methods of engaging with, and influencing, political stakeholders or other senior audiences • Be able to produce clear, concise written communication in a variety of formats for a variety of audiences • Experience of leading own pieces of work to delivery by deadline With excellent communication and relationship building skills you’ll be able to work with colleagues across the BHF, as well as develop strong working relationships with key external contacts in Wales. We are committed to fostering a workplace where everyone feels valued and supported. Embracing different perspectives and backgrounds strengthens our organisation and empowers us to make a real difference together. Closing Date : 28 July 2026 Interview/Start Dates Interviews will be held virtually, via MS Teams, on Wednesday 12 th August. Application Details It’s quick and easy to apply for a role at the BHF. Just click on the link below. All you’ll need is an up-to-date CV and a supporting statement, outlining your interest in the role and how you meet the role’s criteria. https://jobs.bhf.org.uk/vacancies/20313/policy-and-public-affairs-officer--wales.html Our recruitment processes are fair, accessible, and inclusive. BHF use anonymous CV software as part of the application journey. Website : http://bhf.org.uk Additional Notes 16 July: Interviews to be held on Teams not in person
item.dc:creator: British Heart Foundation

item.title: Parliamentary Assistant
item.description: Kate Osborne MP is looking to recruit an organised, politically aware and highly motivated Parliamentary Assistant to join her team. This is an excellent opportunity to support the parliamentary work of an active constituency MP. The successful candidate will undertake research, prepare detailed briefings and help ensure Kate is fully informed on parliamentary, policy and constituency matters. The successful candidate will have excellent research and written communication skills, strong political judgement and the ability to manage a varied workload in a fast-paced environment. Key Responsibilities Analyse, evaluate and interpret data to ensure Member is accurately informed on key issues Assist in writing external communications including speeches for the MP Compilation of briefing packs for MPs, take notes of meetings and draft appropriate submissions Develop and maintain current knowledge on relevant areas of parliamentary business including Motions, legislation, Hansard, debates etc. This would include issues of importance both nationally and in the local constituency. Diary Management Engage confidentially, professionally, with care and integrity with members of the public, through telephone enquiries, social media, email and face to face Have working knowledge of relevant legislation and upcoming business including how they impact on the constituency Help draft amendments for reports and prepare briefing material Maintain positive on-going relationships with all parties including members of the public, groups, suppliers and Westminster where required Prepare and present briefing notes for committees, press releases, parliamentary questions etc. Respond to routine correspondence and enquiries There may be a requirement for the role holder to work outside of normal working hours and travel for work occasionally To advise and ensure the MP is fully briefed on potential questions and motions to be put to the House Undertake research on relevant subjects as directed Additional Responsibilities Arrange parliamentary events and ensure the MP is fully briefed on attendees Liaise with relevant secretariats of APPGs, committees and other relevant organisations Compilation of diary and briefing packs for the MP's work as part of the Council of Europe UK delegation Upon appointment you will be required to comply with the Baseline Personnel Security Standard, undertaken by the Members’ Staff Verification Office (MSVO). See Members’ Staff Verification Office (MSVO) page for further info. MPs generally pay staff in accordance with IPSA guidelines . Closing Date : 24 August 2026 Interview/Start Dates Interviews will take place the week commencing Monday 31 September 2026 Expected start date: October Application Details Full job advertisement can be found on Kate's website: https://kate-osborne.co.uk/vacancies/ Please submit a CV and covering letter explaining your interest in the position and the skills and experience you would bring to the role. Applicants are also encouraged to provide 2-5 examples of relevant written, digital, photographic or video content they have produced. This can be as simple as a Facebook post, or a press release or video content. Applications should be sent to: kate.osborne.mp@parliament.uk with the subject 'Parliamentary Assistant' Website : https://kate-osborne.co.uk/

item.title: Administrative Assistant
item.description: An exciting opportunity has arisen for an experienced and highly organised Administrative Assistant to join the constituency office of Rt Hon Liam Byrne MP on a fixed-term contract until 31 March 2027. This is a fast-paced role within a busy constituency office, providing high-quality administrative support to ensure the office operates efficiently while delivering an excellent service to constituents. You will work closely with the Head of Office and wider team, helping to manage office systems, coordinate meetings and events, support policy casework, and maintain accurate records. Key Responsibilities Manage Mr Byrne's and the team's busy diary, coordinating a fast-moving schedule. Manage shared office inboxes, responding to enquiries where appropriate. Support the management of policy casework and maintain accurate records using the Parliamentary Caseworker system and other databases. Coordinate meetings, constituency surgeries, community visits and events. Provide administrative support, including drafting routine correspondence, processing post, maintaining filing systems and ordering office supplies. Assist with the day-to-day running of the office and undertake other reasonable duties as required. About You The successful candidate will have excellent organisational skills, strong attention to detail and the ability to manage competing priorities in a busy environment. You will be an effective communicator with strong IT skills, particularly in Microsoft Outlook, Word and Excel, and be able to handle confidential information with discretion. You will also demonstrate a proactive, flexible approach to work and a commitment to delivering an excellent service to constituents. Essential Previous experience in an administrative or office support role. Experience working in the public, voluntary or charity sector. Desirable An understanding of the communities, issues and stakeholders within Birmingham Hodge Hill & Solihull North. An understanding of Parliament and the role of an MP's constituency office. What We Offer The opportunity to make a real difference to local people. A varied and rewarding role supporting the work of an elected Member of Parliament. A friendly, supportive and collaborative working environment. Opportunities to develop new skills and gain valuable experience within a busy parliamentary office. Upon appointment you will be required to comply with the Baseline Personnel Security Standard, undertaken by the Members’ Staff Verification Office (MSVO). See Members’ Staff Verification Office (MSVO) page for further info. MPs generally pay staff in accordance with IPSA guidelines . Closing Date : 31 July 2026 Interview/Start Dates Please note that applications will be reviewed as they are received, and interviews may take place before the closing date. Early applications are therefore encouraged, and we reserve the right to close the vacancy early if a suitable candidate is identified. Application Details Please submit your CV together with a covering letter (maximum two sides of A4) explaining how your skills and experience meet the requirements of the role. Applications should be sent to craig.rogers@parliament.uk Website : http://www.liambyrnemp.co.uk

item.title: Account Manager - Health policy and public affairs
item.description: Hinton Advisers is seeking an experienced, confident and strategically minded Account Manager to play a leading role in client work across the UK and global health policy landscapes. This is a pivotal role within our growing team, offering the chance to shape high-impact public affairs programmes and guide clients through complex political and policy environments. Hinton Advisers is a small independent agency offering specialist health policy and public affairs advice to clients across the life science ecosystem. This is an exciting opportunity to be part of a dynamic team and build your career with an agency that’s investing in its people from the start. For full details please visit our website: https://hintonadvisers.com/job/account-manager-health-policy-government-affairs/ Closing Date : 17 August 2026 Interview/Start Dates Applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis, and we may close the process early if we find the right candidate - so we encourage early submissions. First stage interviews will be held virtually in the w/c 31 August and final in-person interviews held in the w/c 7 September in central London. Application Details At Hinton Advisers, we are committed to creating an inclusive workplace and we welcome applications from people of all backgrounds, experiences and perspectives. To apply, please send your CV and a cover letter of no more than two pages outlining why you’re the right fit for this role. In your cover letter, we’d also like you to share your perspectives on the key challenges and opportunities for the global pharmaceutical industry in 2026/27. Please send CV and cover letter to careers@hintonadvisers.com Website : http://www.hintonadvisers.com

item.title: Account Executive
item.description: Total Politics’ Political Engagement unit is looking for a new Account Executive in our sales team to research partnership opportunities, win new business and manage client relations across our unique portfolio of political media platforms and events. Total Politics Group publishes The House magazine, Parliament’s own publication; PoliticsHome , the UK’s most popular political news site; and ConservativeHome , the leading conservative news site in the UK. We also produce award-winning events programmes in Westminster and across the Party Conferences; and sell advertising across our own sites plus Labour List, as part of a fast-growing media and events business. The successful candidate will use their understanding of the media industry and/or political landscape to win new clients, develop existing relationships and generate new revenues, in an environment that offers an unparalleled opportunity for up-close involvement in Westminster politics. Responsibilities Work across our portfolio of market-leading political media and events platforms to secure new business opportunities from new and existing clients; Evaluate & research current developments in Parliamentary and Government business and across Committee submissions to identify business opportunities and client challenges; Develop and engage a network of public affairs prospects, in-house and in agencies; Meet and exceed individual and team revenue targets; Liaise and work collaboratively with Total Politics Group media, production and events departments, to create and deliver client campaigns to a high standard; and Update and maintain company systems as appropriate (CRM, production schedules, etc.). Experience Knowledge of and interest in politics and political institutions. Public affairs and/or sales and client management experience desirable. Experience with sponsorship or digital sales beneficial but not essential. Experience of working in a role with targets and KPIs. Degree level education beneficial but not essential. Closing Date : 31 July 2026 Interview/Start Dates Interviews will take place across August and September, with an expected start date in September/October. Application Details To apply for this role, please go to: https://totalpoliticsgroup.bamboohr.com/careers/96 , complete your details and upload your CV. Website : https://www.totalpolitics.com

item.description: We're looking for an Associate or Senior Associate to join our public affairs team. Anacta works with a brilliant group of clients who are doing really exciting work. We deliver market-leading political consultancy, political intelligence and stakeholder engagement for organisations who need to navigate the ever-changing political landscape with confidence. If you're curious about politics, sharp with analysis, comfortable in a fast paced role and want to build a career at the heart of public affairs, we'd love to hear from you. Closing Date : 27 July 2026 Interview/Start Dates We will conduct informal conversation with candidates on w/c 27th July. Those shortlisted will then be invited to interviews during w/c 17th August. item.title: Research Fellowships 2027
item.description: The Governing Body of Peterhouse, applying the benefactions of the late William Stone, former Scholar of the College, and others, intends to elect a number of Research Fellows, who will enter upon their tenure on 1 October 2027. Candidates must, by 1 October 2027, have passed all examinations required for a first degree at a university with a First-Class Honours classification. They should be capable of performing unsupervised post-graduate research. They must have completed their thesis no more than 18 months before the start of the Fellowship. Furthermore, candidates must be graduates of, or current students at, universities in the UK or the Republic of Ireland The College wishes to encourage applications from a wide range of candidates and the Fellowships are available in all branches of University studies. Nevertheless, those considering applying for a Fellowship at Peterhouse should be aware that candidates will be judged against the very highest academic criteria by assessors of international standing. The College receives a very high number of applications per place and successful candidates demonstrate exceptional abilities, beyond that of a good PhD. Potential candidates could usefully consult their research supervisor or some other knowledgeable person before deciding whether to apply The electors will make a shortlist of candidates who will be invited to submit dissertations or other written work. Some of these candidates will subsequently be invited for interview. The annual remuneration of a residential Research Fellow will, from 1 October 2026, be equivalent to point 35 on the University Scale, at present £32,882*. Stipends will be subject to deductions in consideration of emoluments from other sources. Research Fellows who wish to live in College will be provided with College accommodation free of rent and council tax.** The College will support the Fellow’s research expense claims up to a value of £1,752 and an entertainment allowance of £775*. For international candidates, all visa costs and health surcharges will be covered In the case of a Research Fellow who has not yet taken the Ph.D., the College may pay certain approved University fees. Research Fellows are expected to engage in full time research, but may be permitted to teach for up to six hours a week and will be paid for this. All Research Fellows are allowed seven free meals a week at the Common Table. In addition to pursuing research it is expected that Research Fellows will take a full part in College life and activities. The tenure is three years and in certain circumstances may be renewed. Up to one year’s absence may be granted. For further information on eligibility and how to apply, please click the 'Apply' button above from 7th August 2026 The closing date for receipt of electronic applications is Monday 7 th September 2026. Interviews will be held in College on Monday 7th December 2026. *subject to annual review. ** Note that accommodation-related benefits are taxable benefits under current HMRC rules.
item.dc:creator: University of Cambridge

item.title: Senior Research Fellow and Programme Manager in Taiwan Studies
item.description: Contract: Full-time, fixed-term until 30 September 2028 About the Role The Oxford School of Global and Area Studies wishes to appoint a full-time, fixed-term Senior Research Fellow and Programme Manager in Taiwan Studies. The postholder will lead the Oxford Taiwan Studies Programme, managing its day-to-day operations while developing a vibrant programme of research, teaching and public engagement. They will specialise in Taiwan Studies, with expertise in a relevant disciplinary or interdisciplinary field, including politics, political economy, anthropology, sociology, human geography, economic history, media and communication studies, social policy, or gender and sexuality studies. The role includes developing independent research projects, publishing high-quality academic outputs, organising workshops and conferences, preparing grant applications, hosting visiting scholars from Taiwan, and contributing to teaching. The postholder will also oversee the Programme website and communications, building relationships with stakeholders across and beyond the University. The position is based at the University of Oxford China Centre, Dickson Poon Building, Oxford, and is full-time and fixed-term until 30 September 2028 , starting on 1 October 2026 , or as soon as possible thereafter. About You You will hold a PhD/DPhil in a relevant discipline with a focus on Taiwan or Taiwan in comparative perspective, together with relevant research experience. You will have an established publication record in peer-reviewed outlets and the ability to initiate and manage independent research. You will demonstrate specialist knowledge of Taiwan Studies and the ability to develop innovative research projects, contribute to research income generation, and collaborate effectively with colleagues and external partners. Excellent organisational, communication and programme management skills are essential. We particularly welcome applications from women and Black and Minority Ethnic candidates, who are under-represented in academic posts at Oxford. Candidates who have taken career breaks or followed non-traditional career paths are encouraged to apply and will be considered fairly. What We Offer The University of Oxford offers an excellent range of benefits, including: An excellent contributory pension scheme 38 days' annual leave (pro rata for part-time roles) Comprehensive childcare services Family leave schemes Discounted bus travel and season ticket loans Application Process Further information, including the full job description and selection criteria (Vacancy ID: 187483 ), is available on the University of Oxford website. For informal enquiries, please contact the HR Team at recruitment@area.ox.ac.uk . Enquiries will be treated in strict confidence and will not form part of the selection process. The University of Oxford is committed to fostering an inclusive culture that promotes equality, values diversity and supports all staff and students to thrive. Reasonable adjustments will be made throughout the recruitment process where required. Only applications received before 12.00 noon (UK time) on Monday 10 August 2026 will be considered. Interviews are expected to be held online via Microsoft Teams on Thursday 10 September 2026.
item.dc:creator: University of Oxford

item.title: Post-doctoral Research Associate
item.description: What makes Goldsmith's unique? Goldsmiths is a world-renowned university that has a reputation for rigorous and innovative academic work; creativity has long been our hallmark. Academic excellence and imaginative course content combine to make a place where creative minds can thrive. About the Department Goldsmiths’ School of Media, Communications and Cultural Studies is one of the longest established departments for teaching and researching media in the world. Housed in the award-winning Professor Stuart Hall Building, it has an internationally outstanding reputation for creative and radical thinking and practice. We are committed to a vibrant teaching and research programme that combines theory and practice with interdisciplinary engagement with the latest developments in media, communications and cultural studies. The School is currently ranked 11th in the world (and 2 nd in the UK) for communication and media studies (QS World Rankings by subject 2023) and second in the UK for research assessed as 3* and 4* - with 96% of our research evaluated as 'world leading or internationally excellent' in the latest Research Excellence Framework (2021). About the Role The post-doctoral research associate will work on the international project ‘State-tech relations in the datafied welfare state: Examining the computational transformation of the European Social Model [STATETECH]’ led by Prof Lina Dencik and funded by an Advanced Grant from the European Research Council. The project explores the relationship between digital technology providers and European welfare states in the context of data-driven innovation. The candidate will oversee the work package on ‘infrastructures’ that examines the features and ecosystems of platforms and AI applications that are used by different governments. The candidate will be expected to work collaboratively to map data infrastructures and have the ability to use some data science methods. About the Candidate The successful candidate must have a PhD in infrastructure studies, data science or related fields, and have experience mapping data infrastructures. We are particularly looking for someone who has a good understanding of the technical features of platforms and AI and who is able to use some data science methods to collect and visualize data on government data systems in Europe. Benefits We have generous benefits – an agile working environment, 28 days’ annual leave plus 6 paid closure days (4 at Christmas and 2 at Easter) plus all Bank Holidays, great transport links, a defined benefit pension scheme, support for professional development and a broad range of wellbeing initiatives such as staff choir, running club and creative writing classes. Further Details: Job Description (PDF) At Goldsmiths, University of London, Equality, Diversity, and Inclusion shape how we lead, learn, and work. We are committed to building an inclusive and respectful environment, removing barriers and addressing inequalities so that everyone in our community can belong and thrive. All applicants must evidence their right to work in the UK prior to commencing work with us.
item.dc:creator: Goldsmiths, University of London

item.title: Senior International Policy & Data Analyst
item.description: The British Academy – the UK’s national body for the humanities and social sciences – is seeking a Senior International Policy & Data Analyst to join our Policy Directorate. This key specialist role will develop the quantitative evidence gathering and analysis that underpins the work of the Academy’s Global Strategy, Policy & Engagement Team. The role This is an exciting new role within the Global Strategy, Policy & Engagement Team. You will lead the Academy’s international policy and data work, acting as the team’s expert on quantitative evidence gathering, including primary and secondary analysis, modelling and dissemination. You will identify data, evidence, gaps and trends to support the Academy’s goal of shaping international policy, particularly in demonstrating the value of the SHAPE disciplines, knowledge systems strengthening and equitable partnerships. Working with colleagues, Academy Fellows and external stakeholders across research, policy and practice, you will lead projects within the Academy’s international policy and engagement portfolio. You will take a proactive, collaborative approach to building a strong quantitative evidence base, using data gathering, analysis and visualisation to support the Academy’s international work. The role will work closely with colleagues in the Higher Education & Research Policy team and those supporting the SHAPE Observatory. Working across the Global Strategy, Policy & Engagement Team, you will contribute to programmes covering EU research and innovation, international mobility policy, global research and innovation, global (dis)order, and humanity, climate and nature. The primary focus will be on EU research and innovation, international mobility policy, and global research and innovation. To find out more about the Academy, working with us, our terms and conditions and benefits, and to view the full job description, including the key responsibilities and role requirements, as well as to apply for this role, please visit our recruitment page: https://app.beapplied.com/apply/4rtle5gv1m If you have any questions, please contact the HR team at hrteam@thebritishacademy.ac.uk Closing Date : 24 July 2026 Interview/Start Dates Interviews for this role are currently scheduled for 5 August 2026, but this may be subject to change. Please apply by noon on the closing date. We may close this role sooner if we receive a high volume of applications. Application Details To apply, please follow the apply link to visit our recruitment platform: https://app.beapplied.com/apply/4rtle5gv1m Website : https://www.thebritishacademy.ac.uk/
item.dc:creator: The British Academy

item.title: Senior Campaigns and Communications Officer
item.description: Laurence Turner MP is seeking to recruit a Senior Campaigns and Communications Officer to support his work in the constituency and in Parliament. The successful candidate will demonstrate a record of success in digital or print campaigning in a third sector, political, or trade union context. This role is full time and constituency office based, although the postholder will be expected to work and undergo training in London on an occasional basis and as required. Requests for flexible working arrangements will be considered. Key responsibilities • Planning and executing focused campaigns on agreed issues. • Producing and tailoring videos for a range of channels (ie. long-form, short-form, and for different platforms). • Maintain and develop a number of different social media channels – including Meta platforms and the website. • Working closely with other staff to identify issues of wider interest to constituents in the constituency and in Parliament that are suitable for conversion to campaigns. • Design and production of high-quality printed material, such as newsletters and surveys. • Developing and maintaining press relationships and drafting and issuing press releases. • Other duties as may be reasonably directed from time to time. Experience • Experience of producing videos and print material to a professional standard. • A strong awareness of current affairs and prior experience in a campaigning role in a political, third sector, or trade union environment. • Creativity, self-motivation and enthusiasm. • A demonstrable sympathy and commitment to the aims and values of the Labour Party. Upon appointment you will be required to comply with the Baseline Personnel Security Standard, undertaken by the Members’ Staff Verification Office (MSVO). See Members’ Staff Verification Office (MSVO) page for further info. MPs generally pay staff in accordance with IPSA guidelines . Closing Date : 14 August 2026 Interview/Start Dates Interviews likely in the week commencing 24 August. The start date would be negotiated depending on the applicant's circumstances. Application Details Email a CV, a covering letter (of no more than two sides of A4), and a portfolio of relevant work examples to laurence.turner.mp@parliament.uk by the end of Friday 14 August, and use the words ‘Senior Campaigns and Communications Officer’ in the subject line. Requests for submitting applications in alternative formats will be considered. I am committed to being an inclusive employer and applicants are encouraged to make requests for reasonable adjustments as part of the interview process if they have accessibility requirements. Website : http://laurence4northfield.com

item.title: Press & Communications Officer
item.description: Employment hours: Full time, 37 hours Job purpose The Scottish Liberal Democrat MSPs are growing their team at the outset of a new parliamentary term. This is an exciting opportunity to shape the news agenda and communicate our work from the heart of Holyrood. You will be supporting our 10-strong group of MSPs to get things done for people and make their voices heard. The postholder will be positioned within the Scottish Liberal Democrats’ central team, providing the MSPs with press and communications advice and support. You will secure media coverage that reaches audiences across Scotland. You’ll monitor and respond to breaking news, find and tell the personal stories behind our campaigns, publish the parliamentary party’s own research, and much more. You will see your work on the front pages of newspapers, on TV, radio and social media. The postholder will be expected to demonstrate an ability to understand and promote political positions and policy, they will be expected to build credibility with journalists and political commentators, they will be expected to work quickly and accurately under pressure, and they should have a general understanding of how social media is used in political communication. Key responsibilities and requirements Details on key responsibilities and requirements can be found on the Scottish Parliament's careers page: www.parliament.scot/careers/career-search . Closing Date : 29 July 2026 Interview/Start Dates Interviews will be held in early August. The successful applicant will start as soon as possible. Application Details Apply through the Scottish Parliament's careers page: www.parliament.scot/careers/career-search To apply, please select the advert then click "Apply Online" and upload your CV along with a supporting statement as a single document. Please note that the system does not allow you to upload your supporting statement separately. Your supporting statement should be no longer than 2 sides of A4 and should set out how you meet the requirements listed. Website : http://www.parliament.scot

item.title: Constituency Assistant
item.description: Laurence Turner MP is seeking to recruit a Constituency Assistant to support his work in Birmingham Northfield constituency. This role would be ideal for someone who wants to build up their experience, and a range of skills, in a political setting, and who is motivated by helping people in the community. This role is advertised as full time and constituency office based. Requests for flexible working arrangements will be considered. Main responsibilities • Prepare responses to casework and policy enquiries and take on full responsibility for individual cases, as allocated. • Organise constituency events and take on responsibility for individual projects. • Provide cover during intensive projects, such as large mailouts. • Monitor and respond to social media comments and casework enquiries. • Assist with filming and captioning social media content. • Assist with updating the website. Experience and characteristics • Enthusiasm and high standards of team working. • The role will suit fast learners who can cover multiple tasks over the same period of time. • Good writing skills • Knowledge of GDPR and experience of Casework Management Systems, or a demonstrable ability to learn quickly. • Sympathy for the aims and values of the Labour Party. Upon appointment you will be required to comply with the Baseline Personnel Security Standard, undertaken by the Members’ Staff Verification Office (MSVO).
