item.title: Media & Communications Manager
item.description: The Media & Communications Manager will be the primary point of contact for media enquiries, securing positive coverage of the LCCI’s policy positions and campaigns, and proactively identifying opportunities to further raise the profile of our work. Key Responsibilities Act as the primary point of contact for media enquiries, building and maintaining strong relationships with journalists across national, London, and trade press. Draft and distribute press releases, statements, and reactive lines with accuracy and speed. Produce responses and briefings, including quotes for the CEO, which accurately reflect LCCI’s policy positions. Monitor coverage and proactively identify opportunities to raise LCCI's profile. Horizon scanning across Westminster, City Hall and the wider media environment. Draft speeches, briefings, op-eds, and talking points for the CEO and the Head of Policy and Public Affairs (HoPPA). Prepare senior figures for media appearances and public engagements effectively. Take ownership for all forms of internal and external communications, including supporting communication with Patrons and the wider membership. Support the Policy and Public Affairs team on effective campaign communications, translating policy work into compelling narratives for media, political stakeholders, LCCI Members, and the wider public. Send drafted content to the HoPPA for approval, or CEO where required, in good time, ensuring all enquiries received are responded to. Support the production of LCCI digital content. Produce monthly media reports for the HoPPA and CEO. Contribute to the ongoing development of LCCI's communications strategy. Support Policy and Public Affairs Team events where required. Undertake additional duties requested by the HoPPA or CEO. Please see website for full job description https://www.londonchamber.co.uk/about/about/careers/vacancy-listing/ Closing Date : 30 July 2026 Interview/Start Dates Applications remain open until the closing date. However, please note we may recruit before the closing date so an early application is advised. Application Details To apply for the role, please send your CV with a detailed covering letter outlining why you are suited to this role, to careers@londonchamber.co.uk. For a full Job Description see our website https://www.londonchamber.co.uk/about/about/careers/vacancy-listing/ Website : http://www.londonchamber.co.uk
item.dc:creator: London Chamber of Commerce and Industry

item.title: Junior Press Officer
item.description: Job Purpose We're looking for a self-starter with press office experience to join our PR team as a Junior Press Officer. Reporting to the PR Manager, you will bring excellent written and verbal communication skills and a keen news sense to help us deliver proactive and reactive media activities. Key Responsibilities Press office functions, including closely monitoring the news agenda, seeking proactive and reactive opportunities for CMI to bring our expertise in leadership and management to bear and comment on fast-moving events. Identify media opportunities to raise the profile of our key policies, in-house research and the wider value of CMI accreditation. Respond to phone calls and emails from journalists in a timely fashion. Lead on drafting timely and engaging social media content related to Policy and External Affairs, working closely with the social media to both develop and draft content plans and offer wider support to Marketing campaigns. Develop and build media contacts and keep abreast of developments relevant to CMI. Create and deliver detailed coverage and engagement reports and campaign evaluations. Support the wider team on other ad hoc tasks such as political monitoring, social media monitoring and public affairs support. Person Profile Must have experience of working in a press office or media environment such as journalism A strong grasp of how to craft social media content for varying channels and how to utilise them for effective communications Strong understanding of appropriate use of AI to enhance productivity and content Ability to communicate effectively face to face, in writing, by email, and on the telephone Ability to work to tight deadlines on short notice, respond quickly to fast-moving events and manage own time effectively and productively Strong attention to detail Strong collaboration and interpersonal skills Someone who will live CMI’s values and is professional, passionate, progressive and practical Be resourceful in finding new journalists and publications to contact. Have a desire to build a career in PR and display enthusiasm for learning. The CMI approach is to work collaboratively and flexibly and you may be required to take on additional responsibilities as and when required to support the organisation. Closing Date : 28 July 2026 Interview/Start Dates We are currently accepting interviews, so we may appoint someone before the closing date. Application Details Contact HR Gemma Ardis at gemma.ardis@managers.org.uk Website : https://www.managers.org.uk/ Additional Notes 22 July: closing date amended to 28 July
item.dc:creator: Chartered Management Institute (CMI)

item.title: Events Lead
item.description: ABOUT US Startup Coalition is an independent advocacy group that serves as the policy voice for technology-led start ups and scaleups in the UK. Startup Coalition was founded by entrepreneurs for entrepreneurs and fights for a policy environment that enables early-stage British tech companies to grow, scale and compete globally. JOB DETAILS Run our events calendar end-to-end. You'll plan and deliver the logistics behind everything from intimate policy dinners to conference activations, including our Party Conference presence. That means booking venues, managing suppliers, coordinating catering and AV, and making sure every detail lands on the day. Keep high volumes of output moving without dropping a ball. We run a packed calendar across Westminster and beyond, so you'll need to juggle multiple events at different stages at once, working fast and staying organised under pressure. Build relationships that make events sing. From venues to caterers to partner organisations, you'll be the person keeping every moving part aligned and every stakeholder in the loop. Own the guest experience. Managing invite lists, RSVPs, and on-the-day logistics so that founders, investors and policymakers all have a seamless experience at our events. Chip in on everything else. We run a small operation, so everyone works together to deliver great policy work that helps achieve a policy environment that enables tech startups and scaleups to grow and scale effectively. EXPERIENCE & SKILLS This role is suitable for someone who: is highly organised, with a track record of running multiple events simultaneously without missing details. thrives in a fast-paced environment with high volumes of output and shifting priorities. is calm under pressure and comfortable troubleshooting on the day of an event. is a confident communicator, happy liaising with venues, suppliers, and senior stakeholders alike. who loves politics and technology, and wants their events work to support a broader policy mission. Closing Date : 2 August 2026 Interview/Start Dates Interviews will be on a rolling basis. We reserve the right to interview and appoint before the deadline. The start date for the role is ASAP. Application Details Apply via Linkedin here: https://www.linkedin.com/jobs/view/4441623919/ Website : http://www.startupcoalition.io
item.dc:creator: Startup Coalition

item.title: Communications Officer
item.description: Mike Martin MP is seeking a Communications Officer to join his friendly, happy, and hardworking parliamentary team. This is an exciting role where you’ll deliver content across social, broadcast and print media. You'll be enthusiastic, self-motivated, adaptable and have a ‘can do’ attitude: you'll be responsible for driving Mike's communications across all channels. Key responsibilities: Media monitoring and analysis, propose solutions to drive reach in line with targets Proactively develop relationships with journalists in order to land the right stories in the right places Produce online graphics and video content (shooting and editing), publicising Mike’s work in Tunbridge Wells and Westminster Draft and send e-newsletter to constituents Manage website content and keep the information up to date Take ownership of Mike's social media accounts and use them to demonstrate Mike’s activities in Tunbridge Wells and Westminster Own and deliver local constituency projects Required skills: Political nous: an ability to spot an angle or a story An ability to drive and land communications in a fast paced and sometimes ambiguous environment An ability to consistently produce high quality written, video and graphical content. Extremely organised team player Ability to prioritise tasks to meet all deadlines Familiarity of design software and photo and video editing software is desirable Sympathetic to the aims and values of the Liberal Democrats Location: You can be based in either the Westminster or Tunbridge Wells office. Regular travel between both offices will be required. Upon appointment you will be required to comply with the Baseline Personnel Security Standard, undertaken by the Members’ Staff Verification Office (MSVO). See Members’ Staff Verification Office (MSVO) page for further info. MPs generally pay staff in accordance with IPSA guidelines . Closing Date : 9 August 2026 Interview/Start Dates Interviews to be held as suitable candidates come in. We may hire before the closing date. The start date will be in early September. Application Details Please send a CV and cover letter to: Mike.martin.mp@parliament.uk Please ensure the cover letter is no longer than one side of A4 and the CV no longer than two sides of A4. Longer documents will not be accepted. Website : https://mike-martin.co.uk/
item.dc:creator: Mike Martin MP (Tunbridge Wells)

item.title: Constituency Assistant
item.description: Josh is looking for a Constituency Assistant to join his award-winning MP office team! What you'll get up to will include: Overseeing Josh's inbox, post and telephone correspondence, triaging messages so that they can be actioned promptly and appropriately. Coordinating Josh’s busy constituency diary; scheduling meetings, surgeries and community events. Making sure Josh is well briefed for engagements, supporting visit logistics, taking photo/video content, and ensuring accurate notes with clear follow-up actions are recorded. Acting as a first point of contact for residents, responding to their questions and concerns and signposting accordingly, by phone, by email, on social media, or in person. Drafting and sending correspondence to constituents. Liaising with internal and external stakeholders, including local authorities, government agencies and community organisations. What we're after from you: You know how to get things done You’re hot on deadlines, can bash out a strong email in no time, and aren’t afraid to just pick up the phone to whoever to get a task over the line. You’re comfortable handling routine tasks with consistency and care and take pride in getting the fundamentals right, every single time. You can multitask with ease You’re organised enough to manage lots of simultaneous demands from residents, the staff team and Josh without losing your cool, losing sight of your goals, nor losing your magic touch. You might even be a fan of project management tools like Monday.com. You’re a problem solving pro You’re not fazed by complex challenges and get a buzz from working with others to find solutions. You have the resilience and determination to keep going and deliver the best results for residents. You may have experience working in a people-facing role. You’re tech savvy You’re a digital demon when it comes to diary management, inbox management, file storage and other office systems. You might even be geeky about using AI to boost efficiency. You care enough to get stuck right in You’re invested in our mission to support the people of Eastbourne, and to help Josh to be the best MP he can be. You’re prepared to roll up your sleeves and make a positive difference in the town. Closing Date : 16 August 2026 Interview/Start Dates Applications close: 16 August 2026 (11:59pm) Interviews: w/c 24 August 2026 Start date: ASAP Hours: Full-time (37.5 hours per week). Part-time working may be considered (minimum 25 hours per week). Application Details Complete the application form here to apply: https://bit.ly/ConstituencyAsst Website : http://eastbournelibdems.org.uk
item.dc:creator: Josh Babarinde MP (Eastbourne)

item.title: Parliamentary Assistant and Communications Officer - London
item.description: We are seeking a highly motivated, politically aware and organised individual to join my London office as a Parliamentary Assistant & Communications Officer. Applications from candidates seeking part-time working arrangements will also be considered. Candidates who share Labour values and have a commitment to public service and social justice are particularly encouraged to apply. Key Responsibilities • Conduct detailed research and analysis on a wide range of policy and constituency issues. • Monitor and interpret legislation, parliamentary business, debates, etc. • Brief MP on key national and local developments, ensuring they are fully prepared for parliamentary proceedings. • Prepare briefing notes, questions, speeches, reports, etc. • Draft amendments and provide support for parliamentary reports and inquiries. • Monitor local, regional and national media coverage and identify issues of relevance. • Draft external communications, including speeches, press releases and public statements. • Manage and produce a monthly newsletter using Mailchimp. • Create, edit and publish engaging social media content. • Update and maintain website content. • Develop targeted communications campaigns across a range of platforms. • Monitor social media activity and provide insight on emerging trends and constituent concerns. • Advise on policy issues. • Build and maintain effective relationships with stakeholders, parliamentary colleagues, etc. • Assist with casework and constituency projects as required. • Provide diary management support - London diary. The successful candidate will be: • An excellent researcher with strong analytical and problem-solving skills. • A confident writer capable of producing high-quality documents. • Good understanding of UK parliamentary processes and current affairs. • Highly organised with strong attention to detail. • Experienced in digital communications, social media, website management and email marketing platforms such as Mailchimp. • Strong administrative and organisational skills. • Ability to work independently and as part of a small, collaborative team. • Previous experience working in Parliament, politics, public affairs, local government or a related environment. Upon appointment you will be required to comply with the Baseline Personnel Security Standard, undertaken by the Members’ Staff Verification Office (MSVO). See Members’ Staff Verification Office (MSVO) page for further info. MPs generally pay staff in accordance with IPSA guidelines . Closing Date : 31 July 2026 Interview/Start Dates Application Details Please send CV and covering letter to markus.campbellsavours.mp@parliament.uk Applicants will be contacted by no later than Monday 10 August if you have been shortlisted. Website : http://www.campbellsavours.uk
item.dc:creator: Markus Campbell-Savours MP (Penrith and Solway)

item.title: Caseworker
item.description: Sarah Russell MP is looking for a compassionate, organised person who has strong communication skills and a desire to help people to join her small busy team in Congleton, Cheshire as a Caseworker. The role will suit someone who enjoys variety, is IT literate (Word, Outlook, Excel, Teams, SharePoint), is naturally empathetic and able to work calmly under pressure. A strong sense of justice, an interest in politics and government and the ability to communicate clearly is essential. Funding is available for a full-time post until March 2027. People wishing to work Part-time work will be considered. The preference is for someone who is able to work the majority of the time in the constituency office. There may be opportunities to visit the Houses of Parliament. Training will be given, although previous experience of casework would be preferable as this is a fixed term contract. There may be a requirement for the role holder to work outside of normal working hours, including assisting with events with the MP as requested. Key Responsibilities • You will manage individual cases ensuring they are logged and progressed with all identified actions taken. • You will keep all information and records confidential and in line with data protection legislation. • You will engage with constituents confidentially, via telephone enquiries and emails • You will also draft replies to enquiries about Government Policy • You will be responsible for gathering relevant information and/or evidence to progress cases accordingly. • You will be expected to liaise with NGO’s, Government, Local Authorities and the voluntary sector as part of your day-to-day responsibilities. • As part of a team you will be managing a large caseload of often complex, and sometimes distressing casework that you will undertake in a caring and sensitive manner. • Attend surgeries and constituency events as part of the team. Upon appointment you will be required to comply with the Baseline Personnel Security Standard, undertaken by the Members’ Staff Verification Office (MSVO). See Members’ Staff Verification Office (MSVO) page for further info. MPs generally pay staff in accordance with IPSA guidelines . Closing Date : 28 July 2026 Interview/Start Dates Interview 5th or 6th August 2026 Successful candidates will be notified by the 30th July and given a short task to complete to bring to interview. Interviews will be held in Congleton on the 5th and 5th August. Start Date : ASAP Application Details Please email your application to jackie.macarthur@parliament.uk with the subject line Caseworker- YOUR NAME. Include • A cover letter (1 A4 page max) explaining your fit for the role, interest in working with Sarah, and relevant experience. • An up-to-date CV (2 A4 pages max). • Contact details for two referees and notice period if currently employed. CLOSING DATE 11pm 28th July 2026 Website : http://www.sarahrussellmp.org
item.dc:creator: Sarah Russell MP (Congleton)

item.title: Constituency Research Officer
item.description: Sarah Russell MP is looking for a proactive person who is interested in research, community engagement, policy development and making a difference to join her Congleton-based team as a part-time Constituency Research Officer. The successful candidate will be expected to research and prepare detailed projects between now and the end of March 2027. The ideal person will already have some knowledge of the constituency, be able to conduct desk and place-based research and provide and deliver detailed constituency data and local opinion on national emerging policy, as well as organising events. This will included round table and public meetings . Subjects may include, but are not restricted to: • Housing policy, including leasehold issues; • Fatality reduction of various types, and especially around drowning and rural roads; • NHS provision - including hospices/end of life; dentistry; and adult and child mental health provision. It is likely to involve engaging with bereaved families, and constituents who are deeply distressed. The role is ideal for someone who wants to make a difference. It will suit someone who is able to work independently as well as part of a team, is well organised and has good attention to detail. Ability to engage with local authorities, service providers and constituents will be important. Funding is available for 22.5 hours per week until March 2027 and there will be some flexibility on how and where your work is developed. Job Description • Analyse, evaluate and interpret data to ensure the member is accurately informed on policy matters • Compilation of briefing packs for the MP • Prepare briefing notes for parliamentary questions, committees and press releases. • Undertake research on relevant subjects as directed • Project work – including organising round tables, focus groups and surveys • Have working knowledge of relevant legislation and upcoming business and how they impact on the constituency • Engage confidentially, professionally and with care and integrity with members of the public and others through telephone enquiries, emails and face-to-face encounters Upon appointment you will be required to comply with the Baseline Personnel Security Standard, undertaken by the Members’ Staff Verification Office (MSVO). See Members’ Staff Verification Office (MSVO) page for further info. MPs generally pay staff in accordance with IPSA guidelines . Closing Date : 28 July 2026 Interview/Start Dates Interview: 5 or 6 August 2026 Successful candidates will be notified by the 31 July and given a short task to bring to the interview. Interviews will be held in Congleton on August 5 and 6. Start Date : ASAP Application Details Please email your application to jackie.macarthur@parliament.uk with the subject line RESEARCHER - YOUR NAME Include • A cover letter (1 A4 page max) explaining your fit for the role, interest in working on the topics listed, and relevant experience. • An up-to-date CV (2 A4 pages max). • Contact details for two referees and notice period if currently employed. Applications close at 11pm on 28 July. Website : http://www.sarahrussellmp.org
item.dc:creator: Sarah Russell MP (Congleton)

item.title: Research and Innovation Positions at the Faculty of Arts
item.description: Aarhus University is currently intensifying its strategic efforts within innovation and entrepreneurship with the aim of bringing more research into play through concrete societal solutions and collaborations with established companies and public organisations. This initiative is closely aligned with AU Strategy 2030, in which research-based innovation and entrepreneurship constitute a central strategic focus area. To strengthen AU’s capacity to connect research and innovation, the university management has decided to establish a number of new research and innovation positions across the university. These newly created positions are intended to integrate innovation thinking into the university culture while also creating concrete and visible societal impact and value. You can read more about AU’s innovation initiatives and AU Strategy 2030 on the university’s website. The Faculty of Arts is investing in the development of a strong research and innovation environment through four Distinguished Senior Innovator positions. These four positions may either be full-time positions or converted into twice as many part-time positions. We are seeking candidates who can document experience in translating research into concrete solutions through, for example, innovation projects, collaborations with public authorities or companies, policy development, commercialization, entrepreneurship, implementation in practice, or other forms of research-based value creation. A full DSI position entails spending 40% of working time on research and the remainder on one or more innovation projects. A part-time DSI position similarly entails spending 40% of working time on research, while the remaining time is divided between innovation activities and teaching according to the same norms that apply to ordinary professors and associate professors at Arts. The final decision regarding the distribution between full and part-time positions will be made once an overall assessment of the applicant pool has been completed. Applicants are therefore asked to indicate in their application whether they are interested in a full or part-time position. The special research and innovation obligations embedded in the position are initially time-limited. The obligation runs until the end of 2029 but may be extended by mutual agreement. If the innovation obligation ceases, the employee will transition to a regular position at the department to which they are affiliated. Strategic focus areas At Arts, emphasis will be placed on DSI positions contributing to innovative and developmental solutions within key cultural and societal areas. Until 2030, the faculty has a particular strategic focus on: Welfare and health innovation Security and societal resilience Digital transformation, AI and cybersecurity Sustainability, including sustainability in education Political education and democratic citizenship Digital learning and education Creative industries and cultural heritage Applications within these areas will be of particular relevance to the faculty, but applications within other themes will also be considered with interest. It will also be an advantage if the innovation project has a digital dimension. Each position will be anchored in a department within one of the faculty’s three schools, but must maintain a close connection to Kitchen and the faculty-based innovation hubs and contribute to the development of the innovation environment there. Position content and expectations As a Distinguished Senior Innovator at Arts, your primary tasks will be to: Develop and manage research-based innovation projects with clear societal relevance Strengthen the faculty’s innovation capacity through the development of new collaborations between external partners and students, researchers and academic environments Support the development of an innovation culture in selected academic environments in close collaboration with local communities and departmental and school management Supervise PhD students and students in research and innovation projects Participate in interdisciplinary collaborations across AU’s strategic research initiatives Participate regularly in activities in The Kitchen and the faculty innovation hubs, including weekly meetings and other innovation-related activities Employees in part-time positions have a particular obligation to connect innovation projects with teaching activities at the relevant school Qualification requirements Associate Professor level Strong research profile and documented experience with innovation and external collaboration A significant number of peer-reviewed publications in recognised journals or other publications Experience in research leadership and supervision Experience with interdisciplinary collaboration and participation in cross-disciplinary networks Extensive teaching and supervision experience at BA and MA level, documented in a teaching portfolio including a teaching statement Completion of university pedagogical training or equivalent Experience with career development of junior researchers and development of PhD courses Applicants must be assessed as qualified at associate professor level for the overall content of the position, including the innovation dimension, in order to be considered. Professor level Well-developed research profile and documented experience with innovation and external collaboration Strong publication record including original peer-reviewed publications in recognised international journals Substantial experience in research leadership and supervision Strong experience with external research funding Extensive teaching and supervision experience at BA, MA and PhD level, documented in a teaching portfolio including a teaching statement Completion of university pedagogical training or equivalent Extensive experience with career development of junior researchers and development of PhD courses Significant experience with knowledge exchange Applicants must be assessed as qualified at professor level for the overall content of the position, including the innovation dimension, in order to be considered. Application requirements The application must include: A well-described innovation project anchored in a clear research field/question with the potential to address societal challenges A description of how the project contributes to solving one or more challenges A clear account of how the project will contribute to spreading an innovation and entrepreneurship culture at Arts, and the role the applicant sees themselves playing A description of how the project can support the development or consolidation of the department’s/academic environment’s external collaborations An account of how the applicant will support the innovation culture within their own academic environment, school, and across all three schools An indication of whether the applicant seeks a full or part-time DSI position. Applications for part-time positions must include a description of how the applicant will connect the innovation project to teaching activities at the relevant school The application, including the project section, may not exceed three pages. The application must be submitted in English. Mandatory attachments CV Publication list and at least five articles Teaching portfolio Research and innovation plan outlining the project trajectory and contribution to the academic environment’s development, expected societal value and impact, plans for funding applications, and collaboration with external partners The research and innovation plan may not exceed four pages. Please note that although the application process can be completed in Aarhus University’s system without uploading publications, a research plan or a teaching portfolio, applications that do not include a research plan, uploaded publications and a teaching portfolio (or equivalent documentation of teaching experience and qualifications) will not be considered. Application process External applicants must indicate the academic environment in which they wish to be employed and specify whether they seek a full or part-time DSI appointment. Internal applicants must likewise indicate whether they are interested in a full or part-time appointment. Salary and terms of employment Salary and employment conditions follow the applicable collective agreement between the Danish Ministry of Taxation and the Danish Confederation of Professional Associations. Questions For further information about the positions, please contact: Søren Sindbæk, Vice-Dean for Research, Faculty of Arts Phone: +45 40 87 83 34 Email: sindbaek@au.dk For assistance with uploads or questions regarding the recruitment process: Arts HR Support Email: arts.hr@au.dk Qualification requirements Applicants should hold a PhD or equivalent academic qualifications. Formalities Faculty of Arts refers to the Ministerial Order on the Appointment of Academic Staff at Danish Universities (the Appointment Order) . Appointment shall be in accordance with the collective labour agreement between the Danish Ministry of Finance and the Danish Confederation of Professional Associations. Further information on qualification requirements and job content may be found in the Memorandum on Job Structure for Academic Staff at Danish Universities . Further information on the application and supplementary materials may be found in Application Guidelines . The application must outline the applicant's motivation for applying for the position, attaching a curriculum vitae, a teaching portfolio, a complete list of published works, research plan, copies of degree certificates and no more than five examples of academic production (mandatory) . Please upload this material electronically along with your application. If nothing else is noted, applications must be submitted in English. Application deadline is at 11.59 pm Danish time (same as Central European Time) on the deadline day. Aarhus University’s ambition is to be an attractive a

item.title: Director for the Study of International Relations, Broadly Understood
item.description: The Wissenschaftszentrum Berlin für Sozialforschung (WZB Berlin Social Science Center) is one of the leading global research institutions in the field of social sciences. It unites the social sciences to address society’s biggest challenges. The WZB conducts world-class problem-oriented basic research in the social sciences and connects research and society by engaging with decision-makers in the public and private sectors, civil society and the general public. By bringing together scholars from all over the world with their various methodological and disciplinary backgrounds - including sociology, political science, economics, and law -, the WZB fosters a vibrant and stimulating research environment that promotes interdisciplinary collaboration. Research at the WZB is characterized by long-term projects based on multidisciplinary cooperation and transnational perspectives. The current cross-cutting research topics at the WZB are: Climate, Democracy, Digitalization, Education, Gender, Health, International Orders, Migration, and Work. The WZB is a publicly funded non-university research institute and a member of the Leibniz Association. The WZB seeks to establish a new research department and to fill a permanent full-time position of Director (f/m/x) for the study of international relations, broadly understood. We aim to appoint a world-class scholar in political science, sociology, or economics who will establish and lead a team to implement an ambitious research agenda focused on theory-driven empirical research on transformations of the international order and shifting power relations. Specializations might include conflict and war, international security, contestation and legitimacy of international institutions and norms, transnational politics, international political economy, and global governance challenges, such as climate change, migration, trade, or development. Applicants should be well-established within the international academic community, have a distinguished record of excellence, as evidenced by outstanding international publications and other scholarly activities, and should have a proven track record in the supervision of doctoral theses and career development of young researchers. They should be interested in communicating their research findings to the general public. The new research department should be able to cooperate with others in the WZB’s environment of interdisciplinary social science research. Applicants are expected to present their vision for a long-term social science research agenda in relation to other research units at the WZB. Ideally, the successful applicant will be appointed to a Joint Professorship (W3) at a German university. We value diversity and welcome all applications – irrespective of gender, nationality, ethnic or social origin, religion, disability, age, or sexual orientation. The WZB expressly encourages women or persons with migration backgrounds to apply. Equally qualified applicants with disabilities will be given preference. German language skills are not required. Applications must be submitted by 20 September 2026 via the above ' Apply ' button, including in one PDF file the following items in English language: 1) a cover letter, 2) curriculum vitae, 3) list of publications, and 4) a brief statement on current and planned research. The invitations to job interviews are expected to be sent out in November, 2026. Job interviews will likely take place on December 9-10, 2026. All interviews will take place at the WZB in Berlin, Germany. The position will be filled at the earliest possible date. If you have any questions regarding the job opening, please contact the president of the WZB, Prof. Dr. h.c. Nicola Fuchs-Schündeln, Ph.D. via director.appointment@wzb.eu . For further details about the WZB, please visit our website at: www.wzb.eu .
item.dc:creator: Wissenschaftszentrum Berlin - WZB

item.title: Policy Administration and Communications Assistant (Internship)
item.description: We are seeking a motivated and detail-oriented Policy Administration and Communications Assistant (Intern) to support our team in delivering high-level international meetings and events, as well as implementation of our communications strategy. Key Responsibilities Administrative Support and Event Organisation • Support the planning and delivery of high-level events, conferences, and meetings held at the OECD and other venues. • Maintain and update Business at OECD databases, contact lists, and stakeholder information. • Ensure the accuracy of records and membership data. • Assist with document preparation, meeting materials, and administrative processes. • Welcome and assist high-level guests, business leaders, policymakers, and Ambassadors. • Support the management of invitations, registrations, and follow-up communications. • Liaise with OECD teams, conference support and catering teams to ensure smooth delivery. • Assist with on-site event management and troubleshooting. Communications Support • Support creation of event branding materials (posters, totems, graphics etc.). • Assist the design and finalisation of publications and reports. • Support content creation on digital communications channels (X, LinkedIn, Instagram, and website). • Liaise with the policy team and Communications Manager for photo opportunities at the OECD and various events. • Assist with horizontal communications activities as needed. Required Skills and Qualifications • Currently enrolled in a Bachelor's or Master's degree programme in Business, Event Management, International Relations, Communications, or a related field. • Strong organisational skills and attention to detail. • Excellent interpersonal and communication skills. • Ability to manage multiple tasks and work effectively in an international environment. • Proficiency in Microsoft Office applications (Outlook, Word, Excel, PowerPoint). • Knowledge in using the Adobe suite, and content creation tools (Canva, Adobe Express) is desirable. • Fluency in English; with good knowledge of French. Closing Date : 31 July 2026 Interview/Start Dates Before COB on closing date. Application Details Interested candidates are invited to submit their CV and a cover letter to Business at OECD’s Global Events Manager, Kelsey Ware (ware@biac.org), and Communications Manager, Max Jablonowski ( jablonowski@biac.org ). Website : https://www.businessatoecd.org/
item.dc:creator: Business at OECD

item.description: Please see full JDs here - https://www.ukfinance.org.uk/careers/intern-prudential-reporting-taxation-prt-team https://www.ukfinance.org.uk/careers/intern-payments-and-innovation Closing Date : 21 August 2026 Interview/Start Dates ASAP Application Details If you would like to apply for an advertised position, we therefore request that you submit two copies of your CV. item.title: Intern
item.description: Highgate is a strategic advisory firm that helps clients navigate high-stakes issues and contested environments. Highgate is looking for two interns to help execute and deliver strategies and campaigns for clients. This role is ideal for a motivated, enthusiastic person looking to build their career in strategic advisory, dispute resolution, and communications. As part of Highgate’s Internship Programme, you will gain practical skills through on-the-job learning and an opportunity to develop a deeper understanding of your career interests. To find out more, please see the full job posting here https://highgate.bamboohr.com/careers/26 . Key tasks: • Researching and compiling concise briefing notes in the areas of politics, business and international affairs for existing clients and new business opportunities. • Drafting content including reports, blogs, briefings, social media posts, newsletters etc. that support client work. • Researching and analysing media articles for clients, including daily media monitoring. • Compiling stakeholder contact lists, including media, and writing pitches to journalists. • Attending internal and client meetings, writing up summary notes. • Support the drafting and brainstorming of communications and business strategies. • Ad-hoc administrative tasks. Key Requirements: • Completed an undergraduate degree. • Previous professional experience (typically one to two years) gained through internships, placements or employment in a related field. • Excellent written communication skills, strong critical thinking and attention to detail. • Ability to research and synthesise information within short time frames. • Excellent organisational skills, including managing and prioritising multiple tasks. • Strong work ethic, curiosity, a desire to learn and the ability to work in teams and independently. • A keen interest and a good understanding of politics and international affairs. • Digital and foreign language skills are desirable, but not essential • Candidates must have the right to work in the UK. Employment is subject to successful reference checks. Closing Date : 31 July 2026 Interview/Start Dates The deadline for applications is 31st July 2026. We intend to start interviews no later than two weeks post this deadline. Application Details Please apply by completing the application form here https://highgate.bamboohr.com/careers/26. You will be required to upload a CV and cover letter. You will also need to provide some personal information and answer some questions on availability and right to work. Website : https://www.highgate.ltd
item.dc:creator: Highgate

item.title: Digital Content & Communications Officer
item.description: Alan Mak MP is looking for a hardworking, skilled and motivated Digital & Communications Officer based in his Constituency Office in Havant, Hampshire (with travel to Westminster as required). The role is full-time (5 days per week), not a job share and cannot be done remotely. Experience and skills in video (filming, editing and captioning), social media and producing graphics/digital products is essential, including competency with software such as Abode InDesign, Premiere Pro, Canva or similar. The successful candidate will be responsible for shooting, editing and producing compelling content for use across social media platforms, printed literature and more. You should have a deep understanding of digital storytelling and be fluent in platform-specific formats and trends. The role also requires a flexible attitude and the ability to manage projects and workload. An ideal candidate will be familiar with Alan’s constituency and/or the region, and have knowledge of local issues, but this is not a requirement. Previous experience in a similar role, or experience of working in media, journalism, PR or video editing, is strongly preferred. Main duties include, but are not limited to: • Creating campaigns, telling stories and driving engagement; • Producing video and photo content for social media and website; • Drafting press releases, and supporting on engagement with media; • Producing e-newsletter and other materials on constituency issues and the MP’s work; • Monitoring media coverage and social media channels; • Organising constituency visits and assisting with local projects. Skills and qualities required: • Competency with video and photo editing software (such as Abode InDesign, Premiere Pro or similar), desktop publishing and graphic design, and; • Excellent written and verbal communication skills; • Be politically aware, with an interest in current affairs; • Understanding and experience with various social media platforms including Facebook, X, Instagram and YouTube; • Full, clean driving licence, and access to own vehicle; and • Be sympathetic to the aims and values of the Conservative Party. Upon appointment you will be required to comply with the Baseline Personnel Security Standard, undertaken by the Members’ Staff Verification Office (MSVO). See Members’ Staff Verification Office (MSVO) page for further info. MPs generally pay staff in accordance with IPSA guidelines . Closing Date : 21 August 2026 Interview/Start Dates Interview date: We may appoint before the closing date if the right candidate is identified. This is a rolling recruitment process with applications considered as they are received Start date: By mutual agreement, but ideally within 1 month of appointment. Application Details Please send the following, with “Communications Officer” in subject line, to: alan.mak.mp@parliament.uk Please include: (1) CV and covering letter, including date you could start; (2) Current and expected salary; and (3) Portfolio of your digital content (including at least six examples of videos, graphics, photos and other examples of your digital work). Send Dropbox or Google Drive link NOT WeTransfer link; If you have not heard from us within 10 working days, please assume your application has been unsuccessful. Website : https://www.alanmak.org.uk/
item.dc:creator: Alan Mak MP (Havant)

item.title: Communications Officer
item.description: About Baroness Nargund Baroness Geeta Nargund is a leading campaigner, medical entrepreneur and global advocate for women's health, health equity and gender equality. She spent nearly 40 years on the NHS frontline, including over 30 as Lead Consultant for Reproductive Medicine at St George's Hospital, Tooting. She is Founder and Trustee of the Health Equality Foundation, Pro-Chancellor (Health Equity) at the University of Portsmouth, and Honorary Professor of Women's Health at City St George's, University of London. As a Labour Peer, she champions women's health, reproductive rights, workplace equality, child poverty and the social determinants of health, working at the intersection of public policy, healthcare innovation and social justice. The Role We seek an experienced Communications Officer to lead Baroness Nargund's external communications, deliver traditional and digital media coverage, build media and stakeholder relationships, and develop campaigns supporting her work. You will align closely with the Parliamentary and Research Assistant on parliamentary priorities and public engagement. Key Responsibilities Manage media enquiries and cultivate relationships with journalists, producers and editors across political, national, healthcare and international media. Produce articles, opinion pieces, press releases and briefing materials. Lead social media and digital presence, creating content that grows reach and influence. Develop communications campaigns supporting charitable, parliamentary and advocacy work. Monitor news and politics, spot engagement opportunities, and brief Baroness Nargund ahead of media appearances and events. Provide occasional admin and diary support during busy periods or colleague cover. Person Specification Essential 3+ years in communications, journalism, public affairs or political communications. Excellent writing and editing skills, able to explain complex policy and healthcare issues clearly. Media relations and digital communications experience. Good understanding of UK politics, Parliament and the media landscape. Commitment to women's health, health equity and gender equality. Desirable Experience with parliamentarians, senior public figures, healthcare, charities or NGOs. Multimedia skills: graphic design, video editing or website management. Closing Date : 21 August 2026 Interview/Start Dates Please note that this vacancy may close early if we receive a high volume of suitable applications. Application Details Please send your CV and cover letter to barnettcoxg@parliament.uk with the subject line 'Communications Officer Application.' Website : https://members.parliament.uk/member/5439/career
item.dc:creator: Baroness Geeta Nargund

item.title: International Policy Officer
item.description: As International Policy Officer, you’ll use your positive attitude, your knowledge of the UK and international policy landscape and your ability to support the RCN’s International Policy Team and the wider Policy & Public Affairs Department. This will include identifying and analysing publicly available information and monitoring trends and intelligence to produce reports and briefings on the global nursing profession and issues impacting the internationally educated nursing workforce in the UK. To join us you’ll need to be able to demonstrate that you have knowledge of the UK and international policy landscape and an understanding of the lobbying landscape for our work, as well as experience of stakeholder engagement. You will also need to have experience of carrying out desk research to produce policy and political summaries and briefings and be able to demonstrate your ability to contribute to stakeholder content on policy issues. For further details, please follow the link below: https://itrent.rcn.org.uk/ce1090li_webrecruitment/wrd/run/etrec179gf.open?WVID=43302200Aq&LANG=USA&VACANCY_ID=1348571QWt Closing Date : 11 August 2026 Interview/Start Dates Closing time: 11.59pm on closing date. Assessment & interview date: w/c 31 August (exact date TBC) Application Details Please click the link below for further details and to apply: https://itrent.rcn.org.uk/ce1090li_webrecruitment/wrd/run/etrec179gf.open?WVID=43302200Aq&LANG=USA&VACANCY_ID=1348571QWt Website : http://www.rcn.org.uk
item.dc:creator: Royal College of Nursing

item.title: Finance & Operations Assistant
item.description: Your duties will include: Finance • Process purchase invoices, supplier payments and expenses. • Raise sales invoices and monitor outstanding payments. • Assist with bank reconciliations and maintain accurate financial records. • Support monthly management accounts preparation. • Maintain finance spreadsheets, bookkeeping, cashflow and accounting systems. • Assist with budget monitoring and expenditure reporting. • Support the preparation of year-end accounts and external audits. • Maintain financial filing systems and supporting documentation. • Process corporate credit card reconciliations. • Liaise with suppliers regarding invoices and payment queries. Operations • Be the lead on all HJS physical and online events – working closely with the (but ultimately having responsibility for) Research and Communications Departments to ensure smooth and successful delivery of the events. • Support the day-to-day running of the office, including overseeing facilities and office supplies. • Support health and safety compliance and office risk assessments. • Help organise internal meetings, events and board meetings. • Maintain organisational records and filing systems. • Support business continuity planning and operational resilience. Governance and Compliance • Assist with Board and Committee administration. • Maintain governance records and statutory filings. • Support GDPR compliance and records management. • Assist with organisational policy reviews. • Support compliance with Charity Commission and Companies House requirements (where applicable). Other responsibilities • Support the COO to monitor KPIs, introducing clear metrics for monitoring output and impact month on month. • Support where required the delivery of events and conferences. Candidate must have: • Experience using accounting software (e.g. Xero, Sage, QuickBooks or similar). • Experience using Salesforce (or a similar CRM). • Experience in an administrative, finance (bookkeeping) and operations support role(s). Helpful (but not essential) experience includes: • Experience working within a charity, not-for-profit organisation or think tank. • AAT qualification or working towards an accounting qualification. • Knowledge of UK charity governance and compliance. Closing Date : 31 July 2026 Interview/Start Dates Closes on the 31st July. Early applications are encouraged. Application Details Apply through out website here where you can submit your CV and cover letter: https://henryjacksonsociety.org/jobs/finance-operations-assistant/ Following this, we will be in touch with all candidates on a rolling basis. Interviews may conclude before the closing date. Website : http://henryjacksonsociety.org/
item.dc:creator: The Henry Jackson Society

item.title: Support Officer
item.description: About the role We're looking for a sharp, energetic all-rounder to help run the constituency office of David Williams MP. This is a job for someone who can come in on Monday and take charge of the social media, spend Tuesday helping resolve a resident's casework, and never says "that's not my job." You'll lead our digital presence — content, short-form video, graphics — while sharing the day-to-day work that keeps an MP's office serving its constituents. What you'll do Own and develop the MP's social media and online presence across channels, producing engaging content including short-form video and simple graphics. Help residents by handling and progressing casework and constituent enquiries, and drafting correspondence. Support local community engagement, events and the office's day-to-day running. Pitch in across the office as part of a close-knit team. Who we're looking for Demonstrable digital and content skills — social media and short-form video you can show us. Excellent written English and strong organisation. Warmth with the public, sound judgement and discretion with sensitive information. A genuine appetite to help out. No fixed background is required — we appoint on merit. The ability to travel independently across the constituency, for which a full driving licence and access to a vehicle is normally required. A passion for Stoke-on-Trent is very welcome. Upon appointment you will be required to comply with the Baseline Personnel Security Standard, undertaken by the Members’ Staff Verification Office (MSVO). See Members’ Staff Verification Office (MSVO) page for further info. MPs generally pay staff in accordance with IPSA guidelines . Closing Date : 27 July 2026 Interview/Start Dates Interviews will be held shortly after the closing date. We may interview strong candidates before it, so early applications are encouraged. Start date by agreement. This post is subject to parliamentary vetting, which must be cleared before starting. Application Details How to apply Send a CV and a short covering letter (maximum one page) explaining why you're right for the role, plus one or two examples of digital or social content you've produced, to david.williams.mp@parliament.uk by 5pm on closing date. Please tell us if you need any adjustment to take part in the process. Website : https://davidwilliams.org.uk
item.dc:creator: David Williams MP (Stoke-on-Trent North and Kidsgrove)

item.title: Multiple Vacancies
item.description: Vacancy 1 – Head of External Affairs (1 Post) Lead the external affairs operation, shaping how we engage with Parliament, government, business and think tanks. Key Responsibilities • Lead external affairs strategy and stakeholder engagement. • Build trusted relationships across politics, business and civic society. • Provide political intelligence, risk analysis and senior strategic advice. • Plan high-value engagement, events and external representation. • Support parliamentary priorities, campaigns and policy development. We're Looking For • Proven public affairs, stakeholder or political communications experience. • Excellent knowledge of Scottish politics and institutions. • A confident networker with judgement, discretion and strategic grip. • Comfortable operating under pressure. Vacancy 2 – Researcher (2 Posts) Join our PRU and provide high-quality research, legislative analysis and parliamentary support for MSPs. Key Responsibilities • Produce concise research, policy and parliamentary briefings. • Draft speeches, motions, Parliamentary Questions and lines to take. • Analyse legislation, budgets, statistics and Government policy. • Undertake FOI-led investigations and issue monitoring. • Support debates, committee work and campaign priorities. We're Looking For • Experience in research, politics, policy, journalism or Parliament. • Excellent writing, analysis and attention to detail. • Strong grasp of Scottish politics and public policy. • Able to work quickly, independently and accurately. Vacancy 3 – Press Officer (2 Posts) Help drive our media operation by producing clear, punchy and newsworthy communications. Key Responsibilities • Write press releases, statements, op-eds and campaign copy. • Handle media enquiries and build journalist relationships. • Create sharp social and digital content. • Monitor coverage, identify opportunities and manage rebuttal. • Support campaigns, visits, events and rapid-response communications. We're Looking For • Experience in media, journalism, political communications or PR. • Outstanding writing skills and strong news judgement. • Politically aware, calm under pressure and deadline driven. • Creative, proactive and comfortable in a fast-moving environment. Closing Date : 2 August 2026 Interview/Start Dates Applications will be considered on a rolling basis, and appointments may be made before the closing date. Application Details Please send your CV and cover letter, clearly stating which role(s) you wish to apply for, to: Joe McGurk, Deputy Chief of Staff: Joe.McGurk@parliament.scot. Informal enquiries are welcome before submitting your application. Applications will be considered on a rolling basis, and appointments may be made before the closing date. Website : https://reformuk.scot/
item.dc:creator: Reform UK - Scotland

item.title: Policy and Public Affairs Manager (Manufacturing)
item.description: MAIN PURPOSES OF THIS JOB Lead the BIA’s work in bioprocessing and medicines manufacturing. This growing community of companies is a vital part of the UK industry and essential to UK economic growth. You will provide direct liaison, support and development for them, ensuring representation within the BIA and to a broad range of policy makers and external stakeholders. DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES Community and events • Co-lead with the BioProcessing Community Leader to provide the Secretariat to the BIA Manufacturing Advisory Committee and other member/stakeholder meetings, preparing agendas and papers, producing minutes and supporting the working groups and programmes • Work with the BioProcessing Community Leader and BIA Events team to plan and deliver bioProcessUK and other manufacturing events • Stay abreast of the coordination of the LeaP Leadership programme • Coordinate and grow the wider BIA Manufacturing Community (including mRNA) through engaging and relevant content, communications and events • Lead the BIA complex medicines and manufacturing sustainability work Policy and public affairs • Monitor, evaluate and communicate government and parliamentary announcements and activity regarding manufacturing relevant to life sciences • Lead as the BIA’s point of contact for the Medicines Manufacturing Industry Partnership (MMIP) and providing secretariat support, representing BIA and supporting other BIA representatives in MMIP (members and BIA colleagues) and communicating developments back to the BIA and membership as required • Leading relevant workstreams within MMIP as appropriate • Maintain a network of stakeholders to enable BIA’s influence on manufacturing policy • Work with BIA colleagues, members and key opinion leaders to develop sector positions on government manufacturing policy and programmes, and produce a strong evidence base to support advocacy • Working with BIA colleagues, members and key opinion leaders, develop new policy initiatives to increase manufacturing investments and activity in the UK • Work with colleagues across the BIA to deliver influence through joined-up BIA activities across policy, events and communications • Contribute manufacturing content to policy team outputs such as the quarterly Influence report policy briefings and political events. Closing Date : 12 August 2026 Interview/Start Dates Deadline for applications is 1pm on closing date. Initial interviews will take place during week(s) of 10/17 August, dependent on availability. Application Details Interested candidates are invited to submit their resume/CV and a cover letter detailing their relevant experience, with reference to the job description, outlining their approach to the role. Please email your application to recruitment@bioindustry.org with the subject line “Policy and Public Affairs Manager Manufacturing Application - [Your Full Name].” https://www.bioindustry.org/about/careers.html Website : https://www.bioindustry.org/
item.dc:creator: BioIndustry Association (BIA)

item.title: Parliamentary Assistant
item.description: Job Description: Research local, regional, or national issues to support the MP’s work and write briefings accordingly Deal with complex queries on the MP’s behalf, including drafting letters, speech-writing, and responding to consultations Maintain and utilise knowledge of relevant legislation, including impact on the local constituency Keep up to date with parliamentary business, including debates, legislation, motions and Hansard. Ensure the MP is fully briefed questions and motions likely to be raised in Parliament. Understand current and upcoming legislation and its impact on the constituency. Diary management for days the MP is in Westminster Arrange, attend and participate in meetings as appropriate Provide general administrative support Undertake casework as required There may be a requirement for the role holder to work outside of normal working hours, e.g. attending events with the MP Person Specification: A can-do, dedicated and proactive approach Self-motivated, well-organised and able work to very tight deadlines Personable and able to communicate with people from all backgrounds A high standard of written work Excellent attention to detail Ability to anticipate needs, take initiative, and resolve issues before they escalate Sympathetic to the aims and values of the Labour Party Experience working in a parliamentary office desirable Upon appointment you will be required to comply with the Baseline Personnel Security Standard, undertaken by the Members’ Staff Verification Office (MSVO). See Members’ Staff Verification Office (MSVO) page for further info. MPs generally pay staff in accordance with IPSA guidelines . Closing Date : 30 July 2026 Interview/Start Dates Start date as soon as practical in order to support parliamentary work when Parliament returns 1st September. Initial online interview will take place 4th August, with a second online interview on 14th August. . Application Details We value diversity and welcome applications from candidates of all backgrounds. Please submit your CV and cover letter via email by 5pm on the closing date. The cover letter should highlight how your values, experience and skills meet the job specification. Please send to Jayne Kirkham at jayne.kirkham.mp@parliament.uk with the subject line “Parliamentary Assistant”. Website : https://www.jaynekirkham.co.uk/
item.dc:creator: Jayne Kirkham MP (Truro and Falmouth)

item.title: Summer Intern (two posts)
item.description: The office of David Williams MP (Stoke-on-Trent North and Kidsgrove) is looking for two reliable, practical people to help our constituency office over the summer. You'll be helping us reach thousands of local residents — preparing and delivering letters from the MP across the constituency — and pitching in with practical support around the office. It's a paid, hands-on role and a genuine look inside how an MP's constituency team works. What we're looking for Reliability — turning up when you say you will. Care and accuracy, especially with people's names and addresses. Basic computer confidence — we'll train you on the rest. Discretion — you'll see constituents' details, and they stay confidential. Willingness to be out walking in most weathers. No experience needed and no driving licence required. What matters is that you're reliable, careful and happy to help out. Upon appointment you will be required to comply with the Baseline Personnel Security Standard, undertaken by the Members’ Staff Verification Office (MSVO). See Members’ Staff Verification Office (MSVO) page for further info. MPs generally pay staff in accordance with IPSA guidelines . Closing Date : 27 July 2026 Interview/Start Dates Short, informal interviews shortly after the closing date. Start date by agreement, and subject to parliamentary vetting being cleared first — so please apply early. Application Details Send a short covering letter and CV telling us why you'd be good at this role, to david.williams.mp@parliament.uk by 12pm on Monday 27 July 2026. Please tell us if you need any adjustment to take part. We welcome applications from everyone and appoint on merit. Website : https://members.parliament.uk/member/5151/contact
item.dc:creator: David Williams MP (Stoke-on-Trent North and Kidsgrove)
